title: Fail-closed on wildcard tool expansion failure

comments.author: vibesync
comments.text:
## Problem

When the `*` wildcard in `keep_tools_list` fails to expand (e.g. due to a bad API call like the `user_id` header bug), pruning runs with a nearly empty keep-list and detaches all tools from the agent.

## Solution

Abort pruning entirely if wildcard expansion fails. If we cannot enumerate what the agent currently has, we must not prune.

## Files

- `/opt/stacks/letta-webhook-receiver-new/tool_manager.py` — `find_attach_tools()` wildcard expansion at line 106
- `/opt/stacks/letta-webhook-receiver-new/webhook_server/app.py` — keep_tools_list construction

## Acceptance

- If `get_agent_tools()` returns empty/error when expanding `*`, skip the `find_attach_tools` call entirely
- Log a warning instead of silently proceeding
- Existing tools remain untouched

---
Huly Issue: LWBHK-14

title: 500 errors on webhook processing — Graphiti context injection failing with 400 Bad Request
Add deduplication to context injection pipeline
Unify PROTECTED_TOOLS to single source of truth in toolselector

comments.author: vibesync
comments.text:
## Problem

`PROTECTED_TOOLS` is configured in two places:

1. Webhook receiver: `webhook_server/config.py` → `PROTECTED_TOOLS_DEFAULT`
2. Toolselector API: `api_server.py` → `NEVER_DETACH_TOOLS`

They can drift. The webhook receiver hardcodes tool IDs that may change.

## Solution

Make the toolselector the single authority. The webhook receiver should pass `*` (keep all) and let the toolselector handle protection via its own `NEVER_DETACH_TOOLS` config.

## Files

- `/opt/stacks/letta-webhook-receiver-new/webhook_server/config.py` — remove PROTECTED_TOOLS_DEFAULT
- `/opt/stacks/letta-webhook-receiver-new/webhook_server/app.py` — simplify keep_tools_list
- `/opt/stacks/lettatoolsselector/tool-selector-api/api_server.py` — ensure NEVER_DETACH_TOOLS includes search_documents

## Acceptance

- PROTECTED_TOOLS removed from webhook receiver config
- Toolselector NEVER_DETACH_TOOLS is the sole config for protected tools
- search_documents remains protected end-to-end

---
Huly Issue: LWBHK-15

title: CRITICAL: Webhook receiver container crashed — down 6 days
Graphiti retrieval optimization (1-5)
description: Implement retrieval improvements in webhook receiver: get-memory facts, cached node search, episode_mentions reranker, cross-encoder secondary pass, and entity-centered two-stage retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 optimization tasks completed with rollback flags and live verification in webhook logs.

title: Use /get-memory as primary fact source
description: Replace fact retrieval from /search with /get-memory in app.py. Keep legacy /search fallback on error or empty result. Use cleaned prompt -> Message payload conversion.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook logs show /get-memory calls; fact context renders correctly; fallback to /search works when /get-memory fails.

title: Switch node retrieval to /cached/search/nodes
description: Move node retrieval endpoint from /search/nodes to /cached/search/nodes and preserve existing node formatting. Track cache behavior via /cached/cache/metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Node retrieval succeeds via cached endpoint and cache miss/hit counters change during repeated queries.

title: Use episode_mentions reranker for node search
description: Update node SearchConfig reranker from rrf to episode_mentions while keeping current search methods and safety fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Node request payload includes reranker=episode_mentions and retrieval quality is validated on conversational prompts.

title: Add cross-encoder secondary fact pass
description: Introduce optional secondary /cached/search call using reranker=cross_encoder when primary facts are sparse or prompt complexity is high. Merge unique facts with primary set.
acceptance_criteria: Feature-flagged secondary pass works, deduplicates facts, and can be disabled without code changes.
close_reason: Cancelled: /get-memory Python endpoint already performs reranking; secondary cross-encoder pass is redundant.

title: Implement entity-focused two-stage node retrieval
description: Detect candidate entities in cleaned prompt, resolve candidate node UUID, then run centered node search with center_node_uuid. Merge/dedupe with baseline nodes.
acceptance_criteria: Entity-centric prompts produce centered neighborhood nodes; non-entity prompts follow baseline path without regressions.
